summary: Unused typosquat-shaped dependency `fuzy-jon==0.1.0` smuggled into build and runtime requirements
details: pyproject.toml declares `fuzy-jon==0.1.0` in both `[build-system].requires` (line 2) and the runtime `dependencies` table (line 28), and PKG-INFO mirrors it as `Requires-Dist: fuzy-jon==0.1.0`. The package is a Jupyter AI extension; a search of its source shows zero imports or other references to `fuzy_jon` / `fuzy-jon`, so the dependency serves no functional purpose. The name is not a known Python library and resembles a typosquat of `fuzzy-json`. Declaring an unused, typosquat-shaped third-party dependency in both build-time and runtime requirement tables is the structural fingerprint of a smuggled transitive: every `pip install notebook-intelligence` causes pip to fetch and execute `fuzy-jon==0.1.0`, whose contents are controlled by a separate (and likely unrelated) publisher. The harm to the installer arrives through that pulled-in package, not through code shipped in this tarball.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
